title: Pig vs Human Genome Comparison
text: Write 4–6 sentences describing: - your approach (methods) - what you will deliver (figures, model, dashboard, etc.) - Obesity is a complex metabolic disorder characterized by excessive adipose tissue accumulation that extends beyond energy storage to include critical endocrine functions, such as secreting leptin to regulate appetite and metabolism. The progression from leanness to obesity involves dynamic changes in fat cell size, number, and inflammatory status, orchestrated by specific gene expression patterns. Our research consists of two main datasets: GSE61271 - RNA-Sequencing of lean, intermediate, and obese pigs. As well as, GSE162653 - RNA-seq analysis of human white adipose tissue from normal weight and obese individuals prior to and following 12-week intervention with fish oil or corn oil. Our main method for research is comparing these two datasets side-by-side and finding differences between the RNA expression to find which key genes are involved in the progression of obesity in pig models and humans.

2 Project roadmap (living)

Define outcome + predictors / variables
Exploratory analysis + QC
Method 1 (required)
Method 2 (optional/extension)

title: Results

title: Results
text:
0.2 Figures
Add figures as you generate them. Example:
library(readxl) library(pheatmap) library(dplyr)
data <- read_excel(“data/GSE61271_gene_names_and_GSM_headers.xlsx”) metadata <- read.csv(“data/GSE61271_sample_metadata.csv”)
cat(“1. Initial data rows:”, nrow(data), “”) cat(” Data columns:“, ncol(data),”“)
data\(Gene <- as.character(data\)Gene) data\(Gene[is.na(data\)Gene) | data\(Gene == ""] <- paste0("Unknown_", which(is.na(data\)Gene) | data$Gene == ““))
data <- data[!grepl(“^ENSSSCG”, data$Gene, ignore.case = TRUE), ] cat(“2. After removing ENSSSCG rows:”, nrow(data), “”)
rownames(data) <- make.unique(data$Gene)
data\(Gene <- NULL
data\)Gene_name <- NULL
cat(“3. Columns before numeric conversion:”, ncol(data), “”)
data[] <- lapply(data, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x))) data <- data[complete.cases(data), ] cat(“4. After numeric conversion & removing NAs:”, nrow(data), “rows”)
